skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
skill.summary: Build memorable, production-ready frontend interfaces that avoid generic AI-generated aesthetics.
skill.author: pbakaus
skill.license: Apache 2.0. Based on Anthropic's frontend-design skill. See NOTICE.md for attribution.

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill consists entirely of markdown documentation files containing CSS and HTML code examples. Detected patterns (backtick execution, cryptographic algorithms, URLs) are artifacts of pattern matching on documentation content: backticks are markdown code fence delimiters, flagged terms appear in legitimate design guidance, and URLs are reference links to design resources.

content.user_title: Build distinctive frontend interfaces that stand out
content.value_statement: Generic AI-generated code produces forgettable, templated designs. This skill teaches bold aesthetic direction and production-grade implementation that creates memorable user experiences.
actual_capabilities: Generate production-grade HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with distinctive visual design
Apply modern CSS techniques including OKLCH colors, container queries, and fluid typography
Create accessible interfaces with proper focus states, contrast ratios, and keyboard navigation
Design responsive layouts that adapt across devices using mobile-first principles
Implement purposeful motion design with natural easing and reduced motion support
Avoid generic AI aesthetics through bold directional choices and refined details
limitations: Does not execute or test generated code in a browser environment
Design quality depends on clear articulation of aesthetic direction and constraints
Cannot generate backend functionality or API integrations beyond frontend mockups
Static design guidance may not account for dynamic runtime constraints

use_cases.title: Landing Page Design
use_cases.description: Create a visually striking landing page with bold typography, cohesive color palette, and purposeful animations that converts visitors.

use_cases.title: Component Library Development
use_cases.description: Build a consistent, accessible component system with proper focus states, loading patterns, and responsive behavior.

use_cases.title: Dashboard Interface
use_cases.description: Design data-rich interfaces with clear hierarchy, readable tables, and progressive disclosure for complex applications.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Component Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pricing card component with three tiers. Use a refined, minimalist aesthetic with subtle color accents and clear visual hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Full Page Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a landing page for a productivity app. Target audience is creative professionals. Use a brutalist aesthetic with bold typography and unexpected layouts. Include hero section, features grid, testimonials, and CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Design System Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete CSS custom properties system for a design token architecture. Include color palette using OKLCH, typography scale with fluid sizing, spacing system based on vertical rhythm, and component-specific tokens. Support light and dark themes.

prompt_templates.title: Interactive Dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an analytics dashboard for e-commerce metrics. Include data tables with sortable columns, trend visualizations, filter controls, and export functionality. Apply progressive disclosure for advanced options. Ensure keyboard navigation and screen reader support. Use a sophisticated dark theme with desaturated accents.

output_examples.input: A card component for featured articles
output_examples.output: A styled article card with fluid typography, hover state elevation using transform, focus-visible ring for keyboard users, responsive image with proper aspect ratio, semantic HTML structure with article/heading/time elements, and subtle decorative elements that reinforce brand identity.

A mobile-first navigation using the popover API with light-dismiss behavior, roving tabindex for arrow key navigation, smooth height animation using grid-template-rows, safe area insets for notch devices, and reduced motion support respecting user preferences.

best_practices: Commit to a bold aesthetic direction early and execute with precision across all design decisions
Use modern CSS features like OKLCH colors, container queries, and clamp() for fluid, maintainable designs
Design all eight interactive states (default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, success) for every interactive element
Apply the 60-30-10 rule for color distribution, keeping accent colors rare and purposeful
Test contrast ratios programmatically and emulate color blindness to ensure accessibility
anti_patterns: Using generic AI aesthetics: dark mode with glowing accents, gradient text on metrics, card grids with identical layouts
Removing focus indicators without accessible replacements, breaking keyboard navigation
Using placeholder text as labels, hiding critical form guidance from users
Defaulting to pure black (#000) or pure gray - real shadows and surfaces always have subtle color

faq.question: What makes this skill different from standard frontend code generation?
faq.answer: This skill emphasizes distinctive aesthetic direction and deliberate design choices over generic, templated output. It teaches specific techniques to avoid common AI design patterns and create memorable interfaces.

faq.question: Does this skill work with specific frameworks like React or Vue?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill generates framework-agnostic HTML, CSS, and JavaScript that integrates with any frontend stack. Specify your framework in the prompt for tailored output.

faq.question: How do I ensure the generated designs match my brand?
faq.answer: Provide brand colors, typography preferences, tone keywords (minimal, bold, playful), and reference examples in your prompt. The skill applies these constraints consistently across generated components.

faq.question: Does this skill handle accessibility requirements?
faq.answer: Yes. Generated code includes proper semantic HTML, ARIA attributes where needed, focus states, color contrast meeting WCAG guidelines, and keyboard navigation patterns.

faq.question: Can this skill help refactor existing ugly code?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the existing component or page and describe the desired aesthetic direction. The skill will preserve functionality while applying improved visual design.

faq.question: What if I don't know what aesthetic direction to choose?
faq.answer: Describe your product, target audience, and desired emotional response. The skill will recommend appropriate aesthetic directions and explain the reasoning behind each suggestion.
